scenario: llm-wiki adapter validate → native wiki checks (broken-xref, uncited-raw, missing-description, orphan). Valid pages → []; the orphan page has a BROKEN xref. Mirrors lint/all-types.json's perType shape. SPECIFICATION golden (llm-wiki adapter = Chunk 4).
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED as an adapter — but `akm wiki lint` exists (src/wiki/wiki.ts) and defines the structural checks. Chunk 4 ports them into validate().
specSource: docs/architecture/specs/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §6 (wiki-page validation: 'native wiki validation (§7)'), §7 (llm-wiki row: xrefs + citations), §9 (broken links tolerated → warning). Diagnostic {file, issue, detail, fixed}.
realWorldSource: src/wiki/wiki.ts + src/assets/wiki/schema-template.md 'Lint' section — the deterministic structural checks: link orphans, broken xrefs, missing descriptions, uncited raws, refresh the index
notes: SPECIFICATION golden. Native wiki validation (from the schema template's Lint contract): (1) broken-xref — an xref/link target that does not resolve to an existing page; (2) uncited-raw — a raw/ source not cited by any page's `sources:`; (3) missing-description — a page without a description; (4) orphan — a page with no inbound/outbound valid links. Broken links are TOLERATED (warning, non-blocking) per spec §9.

issue codes (`broken-xref`, `uncited-raw`, `missing-description`, `orphan`) are APPROVED (open question 7 RESOLVED, maintainer resolution 2026-07) — no longer proposed. `detail` strings are illustrative. CODE STATUS: `broken-xref` is code-grounded in the existing `akm wiki lint` (src/wiki/wiki.ts); `uncited-raw`/`missing-description`/`orphan` are APPROVED-BUT-NOT-YET-CODED in validate() (they land when Chunk 4 ports the checks into the adapter).

In this fixture: raw/2026-07-http-rfc is cited by two pages → NOT uncited; http-caching + varnish are well-formed (valid reciprocal xrefs, descriptions, citations) → []; orphan.md has a broken xref (pages/does-not-exist) and no citations → broken-xref (warning).

reservedFiles.note: reserved files are not concepts; validate does not run page checks on them (base checks only, non-blocking).

rawSource.note: cited by pages/http-caching and pages/entities/varnish → no uncited-raw finding.

conceptPage.note: valid xref → pages/entities/varnish (exists), has description, cites a raw source → clean.

entityPage.note: valid reciprocal xref → pages/http-caching (exists), has description, cites a raw source → clean.

issues.detail: warning: cross-reference target not found: pages/does-not-exist (non-blocking; cross-references must point at pages that actually exist — wiki schema hard rule).

orphanPageBrokenXref.note: PRIMARY lint finding: the xref target pages/does-not-exist does not resolve. This page is also a link orphan (no inbound xrefs, no valid outbound + no sources); whether `orphan` is emitted as a separate finding is a Chunk-4 decision (recorded under edgeCases below).

orphanFindingIsOptional.scenario: a page with no inbound/outbound VALID links (orphan.md qualifies once its one xref is dropped as broken)

approvedIssue.detail: page has no valid cross-references in or out (link orphan) — wiki schema Lint check.

orphanFindingIsOptional.note: The `orphan` issue CODE is APPROVED (open question 7 RESOLVED). Separately, whether the adapter emits BOTH broken-xref AND orphan for the same page, or suppresses orphan while a broken xref is present, remains a Chunk-4 BEHAVIOR decision (not part of the code-approval resolution). Recorded default: emit broken-xref only (the actionable finding); surface orphan after the xref is fixed.
